stdout:
I’m starting by mapping the changed files, loading the issue docs, and kicking off validation so I can review against both the spec and the current branch state.

The session folder write was denied, so I’m falling back to read-only investigation and moving ahead with the review itself. Next I’m pulling the diff, issue docs, and validation results.

I’ve got the design context. Now I’m using agents to do the heavier lifting: one to inspect the diff itself, and another to run the repo’s required validation commands end-to-end.

While the background checks run, I’m narrowing in on the likely touched surfaces so I can verify the implementation against the issue’s acceptance criteria instead of only reviewing the raw diff.

The design doc is already pointing to a likely gap: it explicitly requires writable web UI flows, while the current frontend search shows read-only issue detail code. I’m confirming that against the server and API layers now.

The server/client inspection confirms there are no writable issue endpoints at all, which is a strong acceptance-criteria miss for the web half of the epic. I’m sanity-checking the preset side and waiting on the validation agents for any additional blockers.

I’ve got enough source-level evidence for a likely fail already, but I’m still waiting for the automated validation results so I can include any hard test/lint blockers instead of only architectural gaps.

I have the validation and diff review back now. The branch is green on tests, but the review still fails on acceptance criteria and one concrete correctness issue in the new health-check behavior.

## Acceptance criteria from the issue description

**FAIL.** The issue requires both gate presets and writable web UI workflows. The design doc explicitly calls for built-in presets for `rust-tdd`, `python-tdd`, `js-tdd`, `security-audit`, and `minimal` (`dev/active/production-polish-design.md:41-249`), but the implementation only ships `rust-tdd` and `minimal` (`crates/jit/src/gate_presets/builtin.rs:16-115`, `docs/reference/cli-commands.md:1254-1268`).

The web half is also missing. The design requires transition/claim/gate-run endpoints (`dev/active/production-polish-design.md:371-391`), but the server only exposes read-only `GET` routes plus gate-run history reads (`crates/server/src/routes.rs:29-56`). The frontend client only has `GET` helpers (`web/src/api/client.ts:15-131`), and `IssueDetail` only loads/displays data with no mutation handlers or action buttons (`web/src/components/Issue/IssueDetail.tsx:35-156`, `201-260`).

## Prior review feedback

`run_history` is empty, so there is no prior review feedback to re-check.

## Architecture and separation of concerns

No new cross-layer violation is obvious in the implemented preset code: preset definitions live in `crates/jit/src/gate_presets*.rs`, storage access stays behind `IssueStore` (`crates/jit/src/storage/mod.rs:190-212`), and CLI wiring is in `crates/jit/src/main.rs:1486-1710`.

That said, the branch still fails the issue because the required web mutation surfaces were never added to either server routes or the web API/client layers.

## Functional paradigm

Mostly acceptable. The preset-loading path uses iterator-based transformations in several places (`crates/jit/src/gate_presets/manager.rs:81-89`, `crates/jit/src/commands/gate.rs:464-482`, `516-518`).

No blocking functional-style regression stood out beyond the missing feature work.

## Correctness and invariants

**FAIL.** `JsonFileStorage::save_gate_preset` writes custom preset files with a direct `fs::write` (`crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:687-703`) instead of the repo’s required temp-file + rename atomic pattern already used elsewhere in the same file (`crates/jit/src/storage/json.rs:113-114`). That is a direct invariant violation.

**FAIL.** The new health-check behavior derives `project_name` from `std::env::current_dir()` (`crates/server/src/routes.rs:61-64`). The UI then uses that value for the page title (`web/src/App.tsx:71-79`). In Docker/systemd/other deployments, the working directory is often unrelated to the tracked repo, so this can surface bogus names like `/app` or `tmp` instead of the actual project. That is a correctness bug in the changed code.

I did not find a new PID-safety issue or unsafe state/gate transition bug in the reviewed changes.

## Safety

No unsafe Rust was introduced. I did not find any `unsafe` usage in the reviewed paths, and the workspace validation passed.

## Testing

The repo-level validation is green: `./scripts/jit-validate.sh`, `cargo test --workspace`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets`, `cargo fmt --all -- --check`, plus web/MCP tests all passed.

However, the feature coverage is incomplete. `crates/jit/tests/breakdown_tests.rs:166-192` contains a placeholder for preset integration, and `:266-270` contains a placeholder for CLI conflict coverage. Those comments explicitly admit the tests were never completed. There are also no web tests for state-transition buttons or mutation flows because those flows are absent.

## Error handling

The Rust error paths I inspected generally return contextual `Result`s correctly.

On the frontend, the new health fetch silently swallows failures with `.catch(() => {})` (`web/src/App.tsx:73-79`). That introduces success-shaped fallback behavior instead of surfacing or logging the error consistently.

## CLI contract

The new preset CLI surface appears to honor `--json` for `list`, `show`, `apply`, and `create` (`crates/jit/src/cli.rs:686-758`, `crates/jit/src/main.rs:1486-1710`). No blocking CLI JSON-contract issue found there.

## Documentation

**FAIL.** The public preset/storage APIs are documented, but they do not include the required `# Examples` sections. See `crates/jit/src/gate_presets.rs:18-123`, `crates/jit/src/gate_presets/manager.rs:9-101`, and `crates/jit/src/storage/mod.rs:190-212`. That does not meet the project’s public-API documentation standard.

## Jit issue management

`./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed; the reported label warnings are allowed per the review instructions.

The issue dependencies in the provided context are satisfied: all prerequisites are `done` except one explicitly `rejected`, so dependency state does not block this review.

VERDICT: FAIL
---
Gate result: FAILED
